message:
locale_abi: bound caller-supplied C string reads via scan_c_string

The locale entry points accept `*const c_char` arguments (locale
name, category names, numeric-format strings, etc.) that came from
untrusted or fuzz-shaped callers. The previous implementation
walked those pointers via `CStr::from_ptr(...)` which scans until a
NUL byte is found — so a non-terminated argument would walk
arbitrary process memory and potentially read off the end of a
heap allocation before finding a NUL (or segfaulting, depending on
layout).

This adds a bounded reader matching the one already used by
runtime_policy / stdlib_abi for assertion / abort paths:

- New `known_locale_string_remaining(ptr)` helper that delegates to
  `crate::malloc_abi::known_remaining(ptr)` in production builds
  and always returns `None` in `#[cfg(test)]` so the test
  environment does not need to stand up a full fallback-alloc
  registry to exercise the rest of the module.
- New `unsafe fn read_bounded_cstr(ptr)` that:
  * Returns `None` for null input.
  * Uses `known_remaining(ptr)` if the pointer is a tracked
    frankenlibc allocation, so the scan cannot walk past the end of
    the allocation.
  * Delegates to `scan_c_string(ptr, remaining)` (from the shared
    `crate::util` module) which also reports whether a NUL
    terminator was actually found within the bound; returns `None`
    if unterminated.
  * Returns the bytes up to but not including the NUL on success.
- Replaces the small set of `CStr::from_ptr` call sites inside
  locale_abi with `read_bounded_cstr` and propagates the new
  `Option<Vec<u8>>` shape into the surrounding logic.
- Drops the now-unused `CStr` import, since every path now goes
  through `read_bounded_cstr`.

Tracking bead: **bd-z4k96** — "locale_abi: bound caller-supplied C
string reads so a non-terminated argument cannot walk arbitrary
process memory." This commit closes the ABI half of the bead;
fuzz-target coverage for the new invariant lives separately.

No public ABI change; the affected entry points still take
`*const c_char` arguments and still honour the glibc locale
contract for well-formed inputs. The change is a narrowing of
previously-undefined behaviour on malformed inputs into a clean
errno-based rejection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
